title: Airbnb Cleaning Melbourne: How to Maintain a 5-Star Rating
excerpt: Consistent 5-star cleanliness reviews are the single biggest factor in Airbnb ranking. Here's how Melbourne hosts manage it without burning out.
htmlContent:
Cleanliness is the number one factor in Airbnb guest reviews. A single low cleanliness rating tanks your overall score and drops you in search results — often permanently, unless you work to recover it. Melbourne's short-stay market is highly competitive, and hosts who maintain consistent 5-star cleanliness have a distinct advantage.

What Guests Notice First

Airbnb guests don't just assess cleanliness — they feel it. The moment they open the front door, they're forming an impression. Research from Airbnb's own internal data consistently shows that the kitchen and bathroom drive cleanliness ratings more than any other rooms.

The top issues guests flag in negative reviews:

  • Hair in shower drains or on bathroom surfaces
  • Grimy kitchen appliances, especially the microwave and stovetop
  • Smells — food odours, pet odours, mustiness
  • Dust on surfaces, ceiling fans, and behind furniture
  • Fingerprints on mirrors, windows, and screen doors
  • Dirty linen or towels with stains

The Turnover Cleaning Checklist

Bathroom

  • Disinfect toilet including under rim and behind base
  • Clean shower screen and remove water spots with squeegee or microfibre
  • Descale showerhead and tapware
  • Clean mirror streak-free
  • Replace guest toiletries and check soap dispensers
  • Replenish toilet rolls — always leave at least 2
  • Wash bath mat or replace with fresh one
  • Check hair dryer is clean and working

Kitchen

  • Clean stovetop including burner caps
  • Wipe microwave inside and out
  • Clean kettle and coffee machine
  • Run dishwasher if used and unpack clean items
  • Wipe benches, splashback, and sink
  • Check fridge for any guest food or spills — wipe shelves
  • Replenish basics (dish soap, sponge, oil, salt, coffee/tea if provided)
  • Empty and reline rubbish bins

Bedrooms

  • Change all linen — even if the guest only stayed 1–2 nights
  • Check under beds and between mattress and base
  • Dust all surfaces, lamps, and bedside tables
  • Vacuum or mop floors including under the bed
  • Fluff and rearrange pillows professionally
  • Close all curtains or blinds at a consistent angle

Living Areas

  • Plump and arrange all cushions
  • Wipe remote controls and any shared surfaces
  • Clean TV screen with appropriate cloth
  • Vacuum sofa if applicable
  • Dust all shelves and decorative items
  • Check for any guest belongings left behind

Between Guests: The Smell Test

Melbourne's weather means properties can become stuffy quickly. Between every guest, air out the property for at least 30–60 minutes — even in winter. Open all windows, run exhaust fans, and leave interior doors open. An air purifier in the bedroom or living area is a worthwhile investment for frequent hosts.

Avoid heavy fragrance — many guests are sensitive to synthetic scents. A neutral, fresh smell is far preferable to a strongly scented property.

The Economics of Professional Cleaning for Airbnb

Many Melbourne hosts pass the cleaning fee to guests, which means the clean essentially pays for itself. A professional 2-bedroom turnover clean in Melbourne typically takes 2.5–3 hours and costs $180–$250, depending on the property and inclusions.

Compare this to a host spending 3 hours of their own time cleaning, plus supplies, plus the opportunity cost of not doing something else. For most hosts with more than 2 bookings per month, professional cleaning is the more efficient option.

Building a Reliable Cleaning System

  1. Find a dedicated Airbnb cleaner who understands short-stay turnover standards — not just regular house cleaning
  2. Create a photo checklist of exactly how each room should look post-clean (make-up bedding, specific towel folding, toiletry placement)
  3. Build buffer time — don't book same-day checkouts and check-ins unless your cleaner is highly reliable and the property is simple
  4. Do a quality check every 3–4 cleans yourself, or use a co-host to spot-check
  5. Pre-stock supplies in a dedicated storage area so your cleaner never runs out of linen or toiletries
